title: Aspirin: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
to treat fever or pain. 
Take aspirin exactly as directed. Do not take more or less of it or take it more often than directed by the package label or prescribed by your doctor.
Swallow the extended-release tablets whole with a full glass of water. Do not break, crush, or chew them.
Chewable aspirin tablets may be chewed, crushed, or swallowed whole. Drink a full glass of water immediately after taking these tablets.
Stop taking aspirin and call your doctor if your fever lasts longer than 3 days, if your pain lasts longer than 10 days, or if the part of your body that was painful becomes red or swollen. You may have a condition that must be treated by a doctor.
Other uses for this medicine 
Aspirin is also sometimes used to treat rheumatic fever (a serious condition that may develop after a strep throat infection) and Kawasaki disease (an illness that may cause heart problems). Aspirin is also sometimes used to lower the risk of blood clots in some patients and prevent certain problems during pregnancy.
Talk to your doctor about the possible risks of using this medication for your condition.
What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking aspirin, tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are allergic to this medication, any part of this medication, or any other medications, foods or substances. Tell your doctor or pharmacist about the allergy and what symptoms you had. tel
